channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Stealth destroyer to be home for 1st hypersonic weapon on a US warship
item.description: The U.S. Navy is transforming a costly flub into a potent weapon with the first shipborne hypersonic weapon, which is being retrofitted aboard the first of its three stealthy destroyers.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Counting is underway in Ireland's election as 3 parties battle for top place
item.description: DUBLIN (AP) — A marathon vote-counting exercise was underway Saturday in Ireland’s national election after an exit poll suggested that the contest is a close-fought race among the country’s three largest political parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PETER MORRISON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy says NATO offer for Ukraine-controlled territory could end 'hot stage' of war
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An offer of NATO membership to territory under Kyiv’s control would end “the hot stage of the war” in Ukraine, but any proposal to join the military alliance should be extended to all parts of the country that fall under internationally recognized borders, President Volodymyr Ze
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protesters gather for third night of demonstrations after Georgia announces suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Protesters gathered across Georgia on Saturday night in a third straight night of demonstrations against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelensky suggests NATO membership could halt 'hot phase’ of war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky suggested this week that NATO welcoming Ukraine into the alliance could halt the “hot stage” of the war, while Russia would temporarily keep the territory it overtook.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Israel hits alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli military jets hit alleged Hezbollah weapons smuggling sites near the border crossing between Lebanon and Syria, according to officials, who allege the Lebanese militant group violated a ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: In Bolivia's scrappy highlands, proud Indigenous Cholas take the runway by storm
item.description: VIACHA, Bolivia (AP) — In the huddled markets, sprawling farms and pulsing parties of Viacha, a town southeast of Bolivia’s capital, it's typical for women to sport bowler hats, tiered skirts and fringed shawls.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and JUAN KARITA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania's parliamentary vote risks being overshadowed by presidential race chaos
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Hundreds of protesters gathered in Bucharest after a far-right populist unexpectedly won the first round of a presidential race, plunging Romania into turmoil just days before the country holds parliamentary elections. “Better to be dead than a fascist,” one placard reads.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants a Gaza ceasefire deal 'now,' Graham says
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) said that President-elect Trump wants to reach a ceasefire and hostage deal before inauguration day on Jan. 20, Axios reported on Friday. "Trump is more determined than ever to release the hostages and supports a ceasefire that includes a hostage deal.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Georgian protesters clash with police for a second night after EU talks are suspended
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Thousands of demonstrators protesting the Georgian government's decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union rallied outside the parliament and clashed with police for a second straight night on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas attacker opens fire at Israeli bus in West Bank, injuring at least 8: report
item.description: Hamas is claiming responsibility Friday for an attack on a bus near an Israeli settlement in the West Bank, which has left at least eight injured, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israelis are wary of returning to the north because they don't trust the ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: KIBBUTZ MALKIYA, Israel (AP) — Dean Sweetland casts his gaze over a forlorn street in the Israeli community of Kibbutz Malkiya. Perched on a hill overlooking the border with Lebanon, the town stands mostly empty after being abandoned a year ago. The daycare is closed. The homes are unkempt.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK Cabinet member resigns over past phone fraud case
item.description: A member of the U.K. Cabinet has resigned after reports revealed she was convicted in a fraud case more than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Amazon workers in India join Black Friday strike action for better wages and working conditions
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Amazon staff in India have joined strike action calling for better wages and working conditions as the company prepares for one of the busiest shopping periods of the year.
item.dc:creator: SHONAL GANGULY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK spy chief says Russia waging 'reckless' sabotage campaign in Europe
item.description: The head of Britain's Secret Intelligence Service said Russia's war against Ukraine is part of a “staggeringly reckless campaign of Russian sabotage” aimed at Europe.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Bolsonaro floats Trump pressure campaign on Brazil in comeback push
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro called on President-elect Trump to help him in his bid to return to power, suggesting Trump in his second term levy economic sanctions on the South American country.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Iran to begin enriching uranium with thousands of advanced centrifuges, UN watchdog says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran will begin enriching uranium with thousands of advanced centrifuges at its two main nuclear facilities at Fordo and Natanz, the United Nations' nuclear watchdog said Friday, further raising tensions over Tehran's program as it enriches at near weapons-grade le
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chad ends a defense cooperation agreement with France, its former colonial ruler
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Chad's government is ending a defense cooperation agreement with France, its former colonial ruler, to redefine the nation's sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK intelligence chief accuses Russia of 'staggeringly reckless' sabotage campaign
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The head of Britain’s foreign intelligence service said Friday that Russia is conducting a “staggeringly reckless” sabotage campaign against Ukraine's Western allies, and that his spies are working to stop the consequences from spiraling out of control.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Negotiators get closer to agreeing on a plastic pollution treaty
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — Negotiators working on a treaty to address the global crisis of plastic pollution inched closer to an agreement Friday, with more countries saying they want to address the total plastic on Earth.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing Canadian hiker found alive after spending weeks in blistering cold
item.description: A missing hiker was located in the Canadian wilderness after being lost for more than six weeks, according to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll rises to at least 15 after landslides bury 40 homes in eastern Uganda
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — At least 15 people have died and 113 others are missing after landslides buried homes in six villages in eastern Uganda, police said Thursday. Another 15 injured people have been rescued and admitted to Buluganya Health Center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hiker accidentally uncovers 280-million-year-old footprints in Italy
item.description: Hikers were responsible for the discovery of what scientists believe to be a prehistoric ecosystem. The first sign of the ecosystem discovered by the hikers were footprints.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin mulls striking Kyiv with new hypersonic missile that can reportedly reach US West Coast
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says that Ukraine’s "decision-making centers" in Kyiv could be targeted next using a new hypersonic missile that could also potentially reach the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden readies $725 million arms aid package for Ukraine: Reuters
item.description: The formal notification to Congress of the weapons package could come as soon as Monday, one official said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: China releases 3 Americans in prisoner swap
item.description: Mark Swidan, Kai Li and John Leung were freed after years of talks between U.S. and Chinese officials.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: World's oldest man has died in England at 112
item.description: John Alfred Tinniswood was born a few months after the sinking of the Titanic and lived through two world wars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Israel ramps up strikes on Beirut as its leadership prepares to vote on ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: The Israeli military also issued warnings for 20 more buildings in Beirut's suburbs to evacuate before they too were struck
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 31 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two senators urge FIFA not to pick Saudi Arabia as 2034 host
item.description: Democrats Ron Wyden of Oregon and Dick Durbin of Illinois have told FIFA president Gianni Infantino in a letter “we urge you to seek out a host country with a record of upholding human rights.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, NewsNation
